nativeRange.habitat: Hard substrate areas in moderately turbulent waters; subtidal rocky reef zones

summary: Vivid red ornamental macroalgae prized for its flame-like branching thallus; generally kept as a display specimen rather than a nutrient-export workhorse. Color retention tends to require moderate, blue-spectrum lighting.
careNotes: Dragon's Breath (Halymenia durvillei) is a popular ornamental red macroalgae known for its intensely colored, branching thallus that resembles dancing flames — the origin of its trade name. It tends to grow either free-floating or attached to rock; attached specimens have been reported to reach over 30 cm in height. In captivity it is generally considered an intermediate-care species because maintaining its vivid red-to-pink coloration requires attention to lighting: moderate blue-spectrum light is typically advised, as high-intensity lighting has been observed to cause browning. AlgaeBarn recommends temperatures of 25.6–28.3°C (78–83°F), pH 7.8–8.4, and salinity SG 1.023–1.025, with alkalinity around 8–12 dKH. Iron and iodine supplementation may support coloration in some systems. It is considered reef-safe but has been noted as a palatable food source for herbivorous fish (tangs, rabbitfish, sea urchins); it is generally advised to keep it away from dedicated herbivores. Propagation in the aquarium hobby is primarily by fragmentation. AlgaeBarn notes it is sensitive to shipping stress. Mosaic Macros describes the tips as fluorescent orange-hued, consistent with the flame appearance. AlgaeBarn depth data suggests 30–50 m in the wild; however, the species is broadly found across the Indo-Pacific photic zone (1–30 m typical for the genus), so a 1–30 m range is used here as consistent with the family. The 'Super Saiyan Dragon's Breath' trade name (Top Shelf Aquatics) refers to the same species. Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)

additional.name: Mosaic Macros
additional.notes: Confirmed species ID (Halymenia durvillei); minimal husbandry data — described as very sensitive to shipping, flame-like fluorescent orange tips.

additional.name: Top Shelf Aquatics
additional.notes: Confirmed species as red macroalgae for nutrient control and display; noted herbivore palatability. Minimal parameter data.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
